title: Ministry of Jesus
description: Jesus of Nazareth begins the public ministry that stands at the center of Christian faith.

title: Crucifixion of Jesus
description: Jesus is crucified under Roman authority in Jerusalem, traditionally dated around 30 CE.

title: Pentecost
description: Christian tradition marks Pentecost as the dramatic beginning of the church's public mission.

title: Conversion of Paul
description: Paul's conversion becomes one of the defining turning points in early Christian expansion.

title: Council of Jerusalem
description: Early church leaders decide that Gentile converts need not fully adopt Jewish law.

title: Earliest Gospel Written
description: The Gospel of Mark is commonly dated to around 70 CE.

title: Constantine's Conversion
description: Constantine's conversion transforms Christianity's political standing in the Roman world.

title: Edict of Milan
description: In 313 Christianity becomes legally tolerated in the Roman Empire.

title: Council of Nicaea
description: The first ecumenical council in 325 defines key Christian doctrine against Arianism.

title: Christianity Becomes Imperial Religion
description: By the end of the 4th century, Christianity becomes the dominant religion of the Roman Empire.

title: Augustine's Influence
description: In the late 4th and early 5th centuries, Augustine becomes one of the most influential Christian theologians.

title: Great Schism
description: In 1054 the split between the Latin West and Greek East becomes formalized.

title: First Crusade
description: Pope Urban II calls the First Crusade in 1095, reshaping medieval Christianity and the eastern Mediterranean.

title: Franciscan Order Founded
description: The Franciscan movement emerges in the early 13th century and becomes central to medieval Catholic life.

title: Protestant Reformation
description: Martin Luther's 1517 challenge to indulgences sparks the Protestant Reformation.

title: Council of Trent
description: The Catholic Church responds to the Reformation through the Council of Trent.

title: Great Awakening
description: 18th-century revival movements reshape Protestant Christianity in the Atlantic world.

title: Global Missionary Expansion
description: In the 19th century, Christian missionary activity expands rapidly across Africa, Asia, and the Pacific.

title: Modern Ecumenical Movement
description: In the 20th century, many Christians begin new efforts toward cooperation across denominational lines.

title: Second Vatican Council
description: Vatican II begins in 1962 and reshapes Catholic worship, governance, and relations with the modern world.
